docs.title: cq-safety
docs.text:
Python dependency vulnerability scanning with Safety CLI 3.

Safety CLI 3 checks your Python packages against a curated database of known CVEs, helping you catch vulnerable dependencies before they reach production.

Safety v3 — Breaking Changes

safety check is deprecated as of Safety 3 and was unsupported after 1 May 2024. The new primary command is safety scan. All docs here target Safety ≥ 3.0.

docs.title: Why Safety?
docs.text:
A06:2021 — Vulnerable and Outdated Components

The OWASP Top 10 lists using components with known vulnerabilities as one of the most critical web application security risks. Safety automates detection of these vulnerabilities in your Python supply chain.

Feature Description 🔍 CVE scanning Checks packages against the Safety vulnerability database 📁 Project directory scan Scans your entire project tree, not just one file 🔧 Policy files v3 Version 3.0 schema with fail thresholds, auto-ignore, and firewall rules 🔐 Auth-based access Browser-based OAuth login or API key for CI/CD 🚀 CI/CD ready Native support for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and pre-commit 📊 Multiple output formats JSON, HTML, SPDX, text, and screen output 🔨 Auto-fix --apply-fixes updates requirements.txt to safe versions 🔥 Safety Firewall [Beta] Block malicious packages at install time

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
# Install Safety
pip install "safety>=3.0"

# Authenticate (development — one-time setup)
safety auth login

# Scan your project directory
safety scan

# Scan with JSON output
safety scan --output json

# CI/CD scan with API key
safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan

docs.title: This Project
docs.text:
This repository (cq-safety) is a code-quality reference project for the Safety CLI 3 tool. It provides:

  • A full tutorial covering installation through CI/CD integration
  • Reusable GitHub Actions and GitLab CI snippets
  • Policy file v3 templates for enterprise workflows

Use the navigation to explore the docs.

docs.title: Basic Workflow (Safety v3)
docs.text:
# .github/workflows/security.yml
name: Security Scan

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  safety:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install "safety>=3.0"

      - name: Run Safety scan
        run: safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan
        env:
          SAFETY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SAFETY_API_KEY }}

docs.title: With uv
docs.text:
name: Security Scan

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  safety:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        run: uv python install 3.11

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: uv sync --group dev

      - name: Run Safety scan
        run: uv run safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan
        env:
          SAFETY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SAFETY_API_KEY }}

docs.title: With Policy File and JSON Artifact
docs.text:
- name: Run Safety scan
        run: |
          safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan \
            --policy-file .safety-policy.yml \
            --save-as json safety-report.json
        env:
          SAFETY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SAFETY_API_KEY }}

      - name: Upload Safety report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: safety-report
          path: safety-report.json

docs.title: Multi-format Report
docs.text:
- name: Run Safety scan
        run: |
          safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan \
            --save-as json safety-report.json \
            --save-as html safety-report.html
        env:
          SAFETY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SAFETY_API_KEY }}

      - name: Upload reports
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: safety-reports
          path: |
            safety-report.json
            safety-report.html

docs.title: Scheduled Scans
docs.text:
Run a nightly scan to catch newly published CVEs against your pinned dependencies:

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * *"   # Daily at 06:00 UTC
  push:
    branches: [main]

docs.title: Adding the API Key Secret
docs.text:
1. Go to Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions in your repository
  2. Click New repository secret
  3. Name: SAFETY_API_KEY, Value: your Safety Platform API key from platform.safetycli.com

docs.title: Basic Job (Safety v3)
docs.text:
# .gitlab-ci.yml
safety-scan:
  stage: test
  image: python:3.11-slim
  script:
    - pip install "safety>=3.0"
    - safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan
  variables:
    SAFETY_API_KEY: $SAFETY_API_KEY   # Set in GitLab CI/CD Variables

docs.title: With JSON Artifact
docs.text:
safety-scan:
  stage: test
  image: python:3.11-slim
  script:
    - pip install "safety>=3.0"
    - safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan --save-as json safety-report.json
  artifacts:
    name: safety-report
    paths:
      - safety-report.json
    when: always
    expire_in: 30 days
  variables:
    SAFETY_API_KEY: $SAFETY_API_KEY

docs.title: With Policy File
docs.text:
safety-scan:
  stage: test
  image: python:3.11-slim
  script:
    - pip install "safety>=3.0"
    - safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan --policy-file .safety-policy.yml
  variables:
    SAFETY_API_KEY: $SAFETY_API_KEY

docs.title: With uv
docs.text:
safety-scan:
  stage: test
  image: python:3.11-slim
  before_script:
    - pip install uv
    - uv sync --group dev
  script:
    - uv run safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan
  variables:
    SAFETY_API_KEY: $SAFETY_API_KEY

docs.title: Multi-format Reports
docs.text:
safety-scan:
  stage: test
  image: python:3.11-slim
  script:
    - pip install "safety>=3.0"
    - |
      safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan \
        --save-as json safety-report.json \
        --save-as html safety-report.html
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - safety-report.json
      - safety-report.html
    when: always
    expire_in: 30 days

docs.title: Setting the API Key Variable
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Settings → CI/CD → Variables
  2. Add variable: SAFETY_API_KEY (mark as Masked and Protected)
  3. Get your API key from platform.safetycli.com

docs.title: Pre-commit Hook
docs.text:
Run Safety automatically before every commit to catch vulnerabilities early.

Safety v3 and pre-commit

The Lucas-C/pre-commit-hooks-safety hook still invokes safety check (legacy). For Safety v3, use a local hook that calls safety scan directly.

docs.title: Setup with Local Hook (Recommended for Safety v3)
1. Install pre-commit
docs.text:
pip install pre-commit
# or with uv:
uv add --dev pre-commit

docs.title: 2. Add a local Safety v3 hook to .pre-commit-config.yaml
docs.text:
# .pre-commit-config.yaml
repos:
  - repo: local
    hooks:
      - id: safety-scan
        name: Safety vulnerability scan
        language: system
        entry: safety
        args: ["scan", "--detailed-output"]
        pass_filenames: false
        always_run: true

docs.title: 3. Install the hooks
docs.text: pre-commit install

docs.title: CI/CD Stage in Pre-commit (API key)
docs.text:
If running in a CI environment with an API key:

repos:
  - repo: local
    hooks:
      - id: safety-scan
        name: Safety vulnerability scan
        language: system
        entry: bash
        args:
          - -c
          - 'safety --key "${SAFETY_API_KEY}" --stage cicd scan --policy-file .safety-policy.yml'
        pass_filenames: false
        always_run: true

docs.title: Running Manually
docs.text:
# Run Safety hook on all files
pre-commit run safety-scan --all-files

# Run all hooks
pre-commit run --all-files

docs.title: Skipping the Hook (Emergency)
docs.text:
# Skip all hooks for one commit (use sparingly)
git commit --no-verify -m "emergency fix"

Warning

Skipping hooks bypasses your security gate. Always follow up with a tracked issue.

docs.title: Legacy Hook (Safety v2 — not recommended)
docs.text:
# Only use if you are pinned to safety<3.0
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/Lucas-C/pre-commit-hooks-safety
    rev: v1.3.3
    hooks:
      - id: python-safety-dependencies-check
        args: ["--short-report"]

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.7+ (3.11+ recommended)
  • pip or uv

docs.title: Install Safety CLI 3
docs.text:
pipuv (recommended)pipx (global tool) 
pip install "safety>=3.0"

# Add as a dev dependency
uv add --dev "safety>=3.0"

# Sync the environment
uv sync

pipx install safety

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
safety --version
# safety, version 3.7.x

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Safety v3 introduces two authentication modes:

docs.title: Development (browser-based OAuth)
docs.text:
For local development, authenticate once with your Safety Platform account:

safety auth login
# Opens https://platform.safetycli.com in your browser

# Check status
safety auth status

# Log out
safety auth logout

Register for a free account at platform.safetycli.com.

docs.title: CI/CD (API key)
docs.text:
For automated pipelines, use an API key passed as a global flag or environment variable:

# Environment variable (recommended)
export SAFETY_API_KEY=your-key-here

# Or pass directly
safety --key your-key-here --stage cicd scan

Tip

Store the key as a repository secret (SAFETY_API_KEY) in GitHub or GitLab — never commit it to source control.

docs.title: Lifecycle Stages
docs.text:
Safety v3 introduces a --stage global flag to label scans:

Stage Use Case Auth Required development Local dev (default) safety auth login cicd CI/CD pipelines --key or SAFETY_API_KEY production Production systems --key or SAFETY_API_KEY 
# Development scan (uses browser auth)
safety scan

# CI/CD scan (uses API key)
safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan

# Production scan
safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage production scan

docs.title: 1. Authenticate
docs.text:
Development (browser login)CI/CD (API key) 
safety auth login
# Opens platform.safetycli.com in your browser

export SAFETY_API_KEY=your-key-here

docs.title: 2. Scan Your Project
docs.text:
# Scan the current directory (default)
safety scan

# Scan a specific directory
safety scan --target /path/to/project

Safety v3 scans your entire project directory — it auto-discovers requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Pipfile, and other dependency files.

docs.title: 3. Detailed Output
docs.text: safety scan --detailed-output

docs.title: 4. Read the Output
docs.text:
No vulnerabilities found:

  Safety Report
  ─────────────
  Scanned 0 vulnerabilities in 42 packages.
  No known security vulnerabilities found.

Vulnerability detected (screen output):

  Safety Report
  ─────────────
  → requests 2.19.1
    CVE-2018-18074 (CVSS 9.8 Critical)
    Affected: <2.20.0
    Fix: Upgrade to requests>=2.20.0

See Output Formats for JSON, HTML, and SPDX options.

docs.title: 5. Auto-Fix Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
Safety v3 can patch your requirements.txt files automatically:

safety scan --apply-fixes

Note

--apply-fixes currently supports requirements.txt files only.

Or upgrade manually:

pip install --upgrade requests
# or with uv:
uv add "requests>=2.20.0"

Then re-run the scan to confirm the issue is resolved:

```bash safety scan

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: Safety v3 is built around safety scan as the primary command.

docs.title: Global Options
docs.text:
These apply before any subcommand:

safety [GLOBAL-OPTIONS] COMMAND [OPTIONS]
Option Description --stage <stage> Lifecycle stage: development (default), cicd, production --key <api-key> API key (required for cicd and production stages) --proxy-host HTTP proxy hostname --proxy-port HTTP proxy port --proxy-protocol Proxy protocol (https default) --version Show version and exit --help Show help and exit

docs.title: safety scan
docs.text:
Scan a Python project directory for vulnerable dependencies.

safety [GLOBAL-OPTIONS] scan [OPTIONS]
Option Default Description --target <path> . (current dir) Project directory to scan --output <format> screen Output format: screen, json, html, text, spdx --detailed-output off Verbose screen report --save-as <format> <file> — Save results to a file (repeatable) --policy-file <path> — Local v3 policy file --apply-fixes off Auto-update requirements.txt to safe versions --filter <key> — Filter JSON output by top-level key

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Scan current directory (dev auth)
safety scan

# Scan with verbose output
safety scan --detailed-output

# Scan a specific target
safety scan --target /path/to/project

# Scan for CI/CD with JSON output saved to file
safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan --save-as json report.json

# Scan with policy and auto-fix
safety scan --policy-file .safety-policy.yml --apply-fixes

docs.title: safety auth
docs.text:
Manage browser-based authentication for development scans.

Subcommand Description safety auth login Open browser to authenticate safety auth login --headless Print a URL for headless environments safety auth logout Log out of the current session safety auth status Show current authentication status safety auth register Create a new Safety Platform account

docs.title: safety generate
docs.text:
# Generate a default v3 policy file
safety generate policy_file

docs.title: safety validate
docs.text:
# Validate the default .safety-policy.yml
safety validate policy_file

# Validate a policy file at a specific path
safety validate policy_file --path /path/to/.safety-policy.yml

docs.title: safety configure
docs.text:
Set global proxy and organization settings:

safety configure --proxy-host 192.168.0.1 --proxy-port 8080 --proxy-protocol https

docs.title: safety check-updates
docs.text:
Check if a newer version of Safety CLI is available:

safety check-updates

docs.title: Deprecated Commands
docs.text: Command Status Replacement safety check ❌ Deprecated (unsupported after 1 May 2024) safety scan safety license ❌ Deprecated safety scan safety alert ❌ Deprecated Safety Platform integrations

docs.title: Beta Commands
docs.text: Command Description safety codebase init Initialize a Safety Codebase integration safety firewall Manage Safety Firewall package blocking

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text:
Safety v3 uses the following exit codes, making it easy to integrate into shell scripts and CI/CD pipelines.

Code Meaning 0 ✅ No vulnerabilities found (or none above the policy threshold) 1 ❌ One or more vulnerabilities found that exceed the policy threshold 64 ⚠️ Command-line usage error 65 ⚠️ No packages found to scan 66 ⚠️ Failed to fetch the vulnerability database

Controlling what causes a non-zero exit

Use fail-scan-with-exit-code in your .safety-policy.yml to control which severity levels trigger a failure exit code. 

fail-scan-with-exit-code:
  dependency-vulnerabilities:
    enabled: true
    fail-on-any-of:
      cvss-severity:
        - high
        - critical

docs.title: Shell Script Example
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

safety --key "$SAFETY_API_KEY" --stage cicd scan --policy-file .safety-policy.yml
EXIT_CODE=$?

case $EXIT_CODE in
  0)
    echo "✅ No vulnerabilities found."
    ;;
  1)
    echo "❌ Vulnerabilities detected — aborting deployment."
    exit 1
    ;;
  66)
    echo "⚠️  Could not reach Safety DB. Check network connectivity."
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

docs.title: Makefile Integration
docs.text:
.PHONY: security
security:
@safety --key "$(SAFETY_API_KEY)" --stage cicd scan || (echo "Security check failed"; exit 1)

docs.title: GitHub Actions — Fail on Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
- name: Run Safety scan
  run: safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan
  env:
    SAFETY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SAFETY_API_KEY }}
  # Step fails automatically when exit code is non-zero

docs.title: "Not authenticated" in development
docs.text:
# Log in once using browser auth
safety auth login

# Verify status
safety auth status

For headless environments (remote SSH, Docker):

safety auth login --headless
# Prints a URL — paste it into a browser on another machine

docs.title: "API key required" in CI/CD
docs.text:
The cicd and production stages require --key or SAFETY_API_KEY:

export SAFETY_API_KEY=your-key-here
safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan

Get an API key from platform.safetycli.com.

docs.title: "No packages found in environment"
docs.text:
Safety v3 scans the project directory (not just the active environment). Make sure you're pointing at the right path:

safety scan --target /path/to/project

If no dependency files are found, add them explicitly via the policy file:

scanning-settings:
  include-files:
    - requirements-prod.txt

docs.title: "Unable to load vulnerability database"
docs.text:
Safety cannot reach the remote database.

Check internet access: 

curl -I https://platform.safetycli.com

Use a proxy: 

safety configure --proxy-host 192.168.0.1 --proxy-port 8080
safety --stage cicd scan

docs.title: Policy File Validation Errors
docs.text:
If safety scan fails with a policy error:

# Validate the policy file before scanning
safety validate policy_file

# Or validate a file at a specific path
safety validate policy_file --path .safety-policy.yml

Common issues: - Using old v2 schema (version: "2.0") — regenerate with safety generate policy_file - Incorrect indentation in YAML - Invalid CVE ID format in allow.vulnerabilities

docs.title: Scan is Slow in CI
docs.text:
Cache Safety's data between runs in GitHub Actions:

- name: Cache Safety data
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~/.safety
    key: safety-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements*.txt') }}

docs.title: Wrong Python Environment Scanned
docs.text:
Safety v3 scans the project directory, not an environment. If you want to restrict the scan to a specific requirements file, set include-files in your policy:

scanning-settings:
  include-files:
    - requirements.txt
  exclude:
    - tests/

docs.title: Auto-Fix Didn't Work
docs.text:
--apply-fixes only supports requirements.txt files. It will not modify:

  • pyproject.toml
  • Pipfile / Pipfile.lock
  • setup.cfg / setup.py

For those, update manually:

# pip
pip install --upgrade <package>

# uv
uv add "<package>>=<safe-version>"

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
safety --help
safety scan --help
safety auth --help
  • Safety CLI 3 documentation
  • Safety Platform
  • Safety GitHub Issues
  • Support: support@safetycli.com

docs.title: Ignoring Vulnerabilities
docs.text: In Safety v3, vulnerability ignores are configured via the policy file (v3 schema). The old --ignore CLI flag is no longer available in safety scan.

docs.title: Using the Policy File
docs.text:
Generate a default policy file:

safety generate policy_file
# Creates .safety-policy.yml in the current directory

Then configure ignores under installation.allow.vulnerabilities:

# .safety-policy.yml
version: '3.0'

installation:
  default-action: allow
  allow:
    vulnerabilities:
      CVE-2018-18074:
        reason: "Not exploitable — our app does not follow HTTP redirects"
        # Re-evaluate periodically; remove when package is upgraded

Apply the policy:

safety scan --policy-file .safety-policy.yml

docs.title: Auto-Ignore in Reports
docs.text:
You can suppress categories of results from the report (they still scan, just not shown):

report:
  dependency-vulnerabilities:
    auto-ignore-in-report:
      python:
        environment-results: true      # ignore env-level results
        unpinned-requirements: true    # ignore unpinned packages
      cvss-severity:
        - low                          # suppress Low severity from report

docs.title: Blocking vs Warning
docs.text:
Safety v3 lets you set per-severity block or warn behaviour:

installation:
  deny:
    vulnerabilities:
      warning-on-any-of:
        cvss-severity:
          - low
          - medium
      block-on-any-of:
        cvss-severity:
          - high
          - critical

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Always add a reason comment for every ignored CVE
  • Set a calendar reminder to re-evaluate ignored CVEs after package upgrades
  • Never allow critical severity without a security team approval
  • Commit .safety-policy.yml to version control for team-wide consistency
  • Run safety validate policy_file after every policy change

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text: Safety v3 supports five output formats via the --output flag.

docs.title: Screen (Default)
docs.text:
safety scan
safety scan --detailed-output   # verbose

Best for local development and manual review. --detailed-output adds full CVE advisory text.

docs.title: JSON
docs.text:
safety scan --output json
# or save to a file
safety scan --save-as json safety-report.json

Best for CI/CD pipelines, SIEM tools, and dashboards.

Example output structure: 

{
  "metadata": {
    "safety_command": "scan",
    "stage": "development",
    "scan_type": "project"
  },
  "vulnerabilities": [
    {
      "package_name": "requests",
      "installed_version": "2.19.1",
      "affected_versions": "<2.20.0",
      "vulnerability_id": "CVE-2018-18074",
      "severity": {
        "cvss_v3": { "base_score": 9.8, "base_severity": "CRITICAL" }
      },
      "advisory": "Requests before 2.20.0 sends Authorization headers on redirect...",
      "fixed_versions": ["2.20.0"]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Parsing in Python
docs.text:
import json
import subprocess

result = subprocess.run(
    ["safety", "scan", "--output", "json"],
    capture_output=True,
    text=True,
    check=False,   # exit code 1 on vulnerabilities — handle manually
)
report = json.loads(result.stdout)
for vuln in report.get("vulnerabilities", []):
    print(
        f"{vuln['package_name']} {vuln['installed_version']} "
        f"— {vuln['vulnerability_id']} "
        f"({vuln['severity']['cvss_v3']['base_severity']})"
    )

docs.title: HTML
docs.text:
safety scan --save-as html safety-report.html

Generates a human-readable HTML report. Useful for sharing with non-technical stakeholders.

docs.title: Text
docs.text:
safety scan --output text
safety scan --save-as text safety-report.txt

Plain-text format suitable for log files and audit trails.

docs.title: SPDX
docs.text:
safety scan --save-as spdx safety-report.spdx

Software Package Data Exchange format for software bill of materials (SBOM) workflows.

docs.title: Saving and Displaying Simultaneously
docs.text:
Use --save-as to write a file and display screen output at the same time:

# Display on screen AND save JSON
safety scan --save-as json safety-report.json --output screen

docs.title: Safety Policy Files (v3)
docs.text: Safety v3 uses a completely redesigned version 3.0 policy schema. The old v2 schema is not compatible.

docs.title: Generate a Policy File
docs.text:
safety generate policy_file
# Creates .safety-policy.yml in the current directory

# Validate a policy file
safety validate policy_file

docs.title: Full v3 Policy File Reference
docs.text:
# .safety-policy.yml
version: '3.0'

# ── Scanning settings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
scanning-settings:
  max-depth: 6              # How deep to recurse into the project tree
  exclude:                  # Paths to exclude from scanning
    - tests/
    - docs/
  include-files:            # Explicitly include additional requirement files
    - requirements-prod.txt
  system:
    targets: []             # OS-level package targets (advanced)

# ── Report settings ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
report:
  dependency-vulnerabilities:
    enabled: true
    auto-ignore-in-report:
      python:
        environment-results: true      # Hide env-level scan results from report
        unpinned-requirements: true    # Hide unpinned package warnings
      cvss-severity:
        - low                          # Suppress low-severity from report output

# ── Fail thresholds ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fail-scan-with-exit-code:
  dependency-vulnerabilities:
    enabled: true
    fail-on-any-of:
      cvss-severity:
        - high
        - critical
        - medium
      exploitability:
        - high
        - critical
        - medium

# ── Auto security updates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
security-updates:
  dependency-vulnerabilities:
    auto-security-updates-limit:
      - patch           # Only apply patch-level auto-fixes (not minor/major)

# ── Package installation firewall ─────────────────────────────────────────────
installation:
  default-action: allow   # allow or deny by default
  audit-logging:
    enabled: true

  allow:
    packages: []          # Explicitly allow-listed package names
    vulnerabilities:
      CVE-2018-18074:
        reason: "Not exploitable — no HTTP-to-HTTPS redirects in our stack"

  deny:
    packages: {}          # Block specific packages entirely
    vulnerabilities:
      warning-on-any-of:
        cvss-severity:
          - low
          - medium
      block-on-any-of:
        cvss-severity:
          - high
          - critical

docs.title: scanning-settings
docs.text: Key Type Default Description max-depth int 6 Directory recursion depth exclude list [] Paths to exclude include-files list [] Extra files to scan

docs.title: report.dependency-vulnerabilities.auto-ignore-in-report
docs.text: Key Type Description python.environment-results bool Suppress environment-level results python.unpinned-requirements bool Suppress unpinned package warnings cvss-severity list Severity levels to hide from report

docs.title: fail-scan-with-exit-code.dependency-vulnerabilities.fail-on-any-of
docs.text: Key Values cvss-severity critical, high, medium, low exploitability critical, high, medium, low

docs.title: security-updates.dependency-vulnerabilities.auto-security-updates-limit
docs.text: Value Description patch Allow only patch version bumps minor Allow patch and minor version bumps major Allow any version bump

docs.title: installation.allow.vulnerabilities
docs.text: Map of CVE IDs to ignore entries. Include a reason comment for auditability.

docs.title: Using the Policy File
docs.text:
# Apply during a scan
safety scan --policy-file .safety-policy.yml

# Validate the policy syntax
safety validate policy_file

# Validate a policy at a specific path
safety validate policy_file --path /path/to/.safety-policy.yml

Tip

Commit .safety-policy.yml to version control. Safety Platform policies will override local policy files if you're using the Safety Platform integration.

docs.title: Scanning Options
docs.text:
Safety v3 — safety check is deprecated

Use safety scan for all new workflows. safety check is unsupported beyond 1 May 2024.

docs.title: Primary Command: safety scan
docs.text: safety [GLOBAL-OPTIONS] scan [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Global Options
docs.text: Flag Description --stage development Label scan as development (default, uses browser auth) --stage cicd Label scan as CI/CD (requires API key) --stage production Label scan as production (requires API key) --key <api-key> API key for CI/CD or production scans --proxy-host Proxy hostname --proxy-port Proxy port --proxy-protocol Proxy protocol (default: https)

docs.title: Scan Options
docs.text: Flag Description --target <path> Project directory to scan (default: current directory) --output <format> Output format: screen, json, html, text, spdx --detailed-output Verbose report (screen output only) --save-as <format> <file> Save results to a file in addition to regular output --policy-file <path> Apply a local v3 policy file --apply-fixes Auto-update requirements.txt files to safe versions --filter <key> Filter JSON output by top-level key

docs.title: Scan current project directory (dev)
docs.text:
safety auth login     # one-time setup
safety scan

docs.title: Scan a specific directory
docs.text: safety scan --target /path/to/project

docs.title: Scan with verbose details
docs.text: safety scan --detailed-output

docs.title: CI/CD scan with API key
docs.text: safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan

docs.title: Save results to JSON
docs.text: safety scan --save-as json safety-report.json

docs.title: Save results to multiple formats
docs.text:
safety scan \
  --save-as json safety-report.json \
  --output screen

docs.title: Auto-fix vulnerable requirements.txt
docs.text: safety scan --apply-fixes

docs.title: Scan with a custom policy
docs.text: safety scan --policy-file .safety-policy.yml

docs.title: Using uv run
docs.text:
If your project uses uv, prefix commands with uv run:

uv run safety scan
uv run safety --key $SAFETY_API_KEY --stage cicd scan

docs.title: Legacy: safety check (Deprecated)
docs.text:
Deprecated — do not use in new projects

safety check was removed from active support after 1 May 2024. Migrate to safety scan.

# OLD — deprecated
safety check -r requirements.txt

# NEW — Safety v3
safety scan --target .
